description: A referendum held on October 2, 2016, regarding the European Union's mandatory migrant quota system. Although 98% of voters supported the government's opposition to the quotas, the referendum was declared invalid due to low turnout failing to meet the 50% threshold.

key_events.description: PM Viktor Orban announces the referendum date.
EU Commission issues stern warning regarding legal implications of the vote.
Main opposition parties call for an electoral boycott to suppress turnout.

calibration_notes: Ideal for studying the disconnect between polling of sentiment versus institutional turnout requirements.
